panel_caffeine.objective: unweighted concentration-scale SSE, least-squares nuisance level
panel_caffeine.parameter_basis: log (u=ln rate, v=ln c_s0); matched-mass 40 g cups
panel_caffeine.verdict: (c_s0, rate) is PRACTICALLY NON-IDENTIFIABLE from single-grind matched-mass whole-cup data. Log-parameter SSE-Hessian condition number 1927 with an inverse-curvature coupling -0.99 (~ -1, the valley direction; a geometric diagnostic, NOT a statistical correlation). The objective-based evidence (conditional on the model and observation operator) is the profile: the SSE (optimised over c_s0) stays within 10% of the minimum over 76% of the swept log-rate grid [0.38, 6.50] (upper extent RIGHT-CENSORED by the tested domain) -> the data do not bound the rate; the MAPE tolerance set overlaps the SSE set with Jaccard 0.86. The best rate sits at an interior stationary point.

panel_trigonelline.objective: unweighted concentration-scale SSE, least-squares nuisance level
panel_trigonelline.parameter_basis: log (u=ln rate, v=ln c_s0); matched-mass 40 g cups
panel_trigonelline.verdict: (c_s0, rate) is PRACTICALLY NON-IDENTIFIABLE from single-grind matched-mass whole-cup data. Log-parameter SSE-Hessian condition number 3619 with an inverse-curvature coupling -0.84 (~ -1, the valley direction; a geometric diagnostic, NOT a statistical correlation). The objective-based evidence (conditional on the model and observation operator) is the profile: the SSE (optimised over c_s0) stays within 10% of the minimum over 45% of the swept log-rate grid [1.29, 6.50] (upper extent RIGHT-CENSORED by the tested domain) -> the data do not bound the rate; the MAPE tolerance set overlaps the SSE set with Jaccard 0.81. The best rate sits at an interior stationary point.

transfer.verdict: At MATCHED 40 g cups the O-refit produces held-out C/F absolute MAPE of ~7-18% (C) and ~3-9% (F) at the point optimum -- a large improvement over the pre-correction fixed-25s result (~25-49%), an unmatched-endpoint artifact (review B1/B5). NULL-BENCHMARK CAVEAT (review A3-01): these ABSOLUTE errors do NOT by themselves establish mechanistic transfer skill -- an O-trained level-only constant is nearly as accurate; see transfer_skill_vs_baselines for the skill scores. MANIFOLD SET (review A2-02/A3-02): transferring the DISCRETE near-optimal O-grind MAPE set (within 10% of the min on the 18-point rate grid) to C/F, the worst AGGREGATE held-out MAPE across the set is 21.7% (vs 18.2% at the point optimum) -- so the aggregate error is stable across the set, though condition-wise prediction envelopes remain owed (A3-11). The (rate,c_s0) split remains DEGENERATE within a grind (the rate flips across flow-map/domain; see identifiability_panel).

joint.pooled_definition: macro-average across the 6 (variety x solute) joint fits
joint.verdict: IN-SAMPLE shared-parameter compatibility (matched-mass 40 g cups, exact weighted-median level, wide log rate domain): forcing a single shared (c_s0, rate) across O+C+F costs 1.5 pp of in-sample MAPE vs the more flexible per-grind fits (pooled 6.4% vs 4.9%). This is an IN-SAMPLE parameter-sharing penalty, NOT a held-out transfer test; whether it counts as 'adequate' depends on reduced-model baselines (a per-grind constant is nearly competitive), not on an absolute cutoff. 0/6 rates at the widened domain boundary.

loco.verdict: Leave-one-condition-out holdout (9 folds/fit, matched mass) gives pooled held-out MAPE 6.5% (median 5.2%). A DESCRIPTIVE residual-resampling interval (ignoring fold dependence, NOT a confidence interval) is [5.0, 8.2]; a condition-level resampling of the 9 (T,p) macro errors gives [5.1, 8.3] around a 6.5% macro mean -- also DESCRIPTIVE (it resamples fold errors without repeating the fit and the LOCO training sets overlap, so it is not coverage-calibrated and does not correct all dependence). Under a LOG (relative-error) level loss the pooled mean is 7.0%, robust to the loss function (M6).
loco.strength: internal leave-one-condition-out holdout + DESCRIPTIVE residual + condition-level resampling summaries + loss sensitivity (within-rig; not coverage-calibrated)

positive_control.verdict: On pannusch's calibration data, fraction scoring LOCALIZES the rate profile more sharply (range ratio ~3-4x, min near rate 1 where pannusch was fit) than the SAMPLED-FRACTION AGGREGATE endpoint (~1.2-1.4x). This is IN-SAMPLE verification of information content, NOT an independent identification of the physical rate; the aggregate is not a true cup (see sampled_aggregate_vs_actual_cup).
positive_control.strength: verification (in-sample, on pannusch's own fit data); NOT independent identification

full_cup_sim.verdict: SAME-MODEL best-case simulation (inverse crime; no model discrepancy): over 20 noise seeds, fine fraction-curve scoring localizes rate=1 far more sharply (range ratio ~14x) than the EXACT whole-cup integral (~2x) -- the aggregated cup carries less rate information than the resolved fractions, within this same-model design. NOT evidence about real cups or model correctness.
full_cup_sim.strength: same-model best-case simulation (inverse crime; seeded noise distribution); NOT an empirical positive control

refit_summary.strength: reconstruction (on-grid, a new calibration); 2-point off-grid O holdout = weak internal check
refit_summary.note: matched-mass (40 g) cups, exact weighted-median level, wide log rate domain. HEADLINE macro-average is the three NAMED solutes (caffeine/trigonelline/5-CQA); TDS is a source-specific aggregate-solids PROXY reported separately, NOT a 4th solute (M5). Refit is a NEW angeloni calibration (granulometry O only, 9-point fit); read per-species rate/c_s0 as a valley point, NOT a mechanistic decomposition. 3/6 named-solute rates at the (widened) domain boundary.

species_bracket.note: pannusch (multi-solute kinetics) brackets the three named solutes + the aggregate-solids proxy TDS in the angeloni envelope, near the low edge -- vs cameron's TDS reading low. Envelope is wide (66 shots x conditions x variety) so this is a regime check, not a per-condition validation.

per_condition.strength: independent, per-condition (granulometry O on-grid; p->flow Darcy q~p/mu(T))
per_condition.note: per-condition MAPE >> pooled-envelope bracket and angeloni's own ~9-13% model, at MATCHED 40 g cups. Inventory-matching helps caffeine, HURTS trigonelline -> not pure inventory; the per-species (T,p) transfer residual is NOT REMOVED by the tested flow maps + inventory match (competing sources -- grain geometry, viscosity, assay -- are not separately bounded here).

flow_map_refinement.residual_note: not removed by the two tested flow maps; competing sources not separately bounded
flow_map_refinement.note: refined Darcy(p,T) flow map cuts overall blind MAPE 23.1% -> 22.6% (closes 0.5 pp of the residence-time component) at matched 40 g cups; the residual >> angeloni's ~9-13% is NOT REMOVED by the two tested maps -- attribution to inventory+kinetics is not established until geometry/viscosity/endpoint are separately bounded.

geometry_sensitivity.verdict: Re-running the frozen O->C/F transfer under each of the three pannusch fitted geometries (1.4/1.7/2.0) applied GLOBALLY moves the held-out C/F MAPE by at most 0.60 pp. This shows only LIMITED sensitivity to the tested GLOBAL Pannusch geometry choice; it does NOT test a grind-specific cross-grinder geometry map (O/C/F -> different Pannusch geometries), which is unavailable (rule 9/A3-20). So the residual is not highly sensitive to the tested global geometry setting -- not that it is broadly geometry-independent.
geometry_sensitivity.strength: global-geometry sensitivity (sweep of the fitted grind geometries applied to all grinds); NOT a cross-grinder map test

sampled_aggregate_audit.verdict: the six-window duration-weighted aggregate differs from the actual BR-1/3 cup by ~28-38% MAPE (ratio ~1.3) -> it is NOT a whole cup; the §6 endpoint score is a SAMPLED-FRACTION AGGREGATE. The repo data have only 6 fraction windows (1,2,3,5,7,10) -- the intermediate windows are absent -- so an empirical complete-shot reconstruction is DATA-BLOCKED; the exact-integral SIMULATION below (full_cup_simulation_identifiability) tests the claim without that artifact.

external_waszkiewicz.source: Waszkiewicz et al. 2026 Phys. Fluids 38, 063113 (repo waszkiewicz2025)
external_waszkiewicz.observable: aggregate-solids proxy (optical TDS); NOT named solutes
external_waszkiewicz.verdict: Target-profiled external SHAPE test (review A3-06/A3-23): with the MEASURED 9-bar flow trace, the 12-fraction TDS trajectory produces a SHALLOW rate-dependent MAPE minimum (range ratio only 2.1x, best rate 0.4) but the minimum MAPE stays HIGH at ~26.8% -- so this is evidence that temporal shape moves the objective more than a single aggregate under the tested operator, NOT a validated rate estimate. The single integrated cup is flat (range ratio ~1.0x) ALGEBRAICALLY -- one scalar paired with one profiled level is exactly matchable at every rate -- so this is not an empirical no-information finding. A target-specific level is profiled, so absolute concentration is NOT tested; aggregate TDS proxy, one coffee/grind, NOT named-solute identification.
external_waszkiewicz.strength: external-data shape-objective localization (target-specific level profiled; shallow high-residual minimum); a target-profiled shape test, NOT a frozen absolute-concentration prediction and NOT named-solute identification

external_waszkiewicz_sensitivity.verdict: Nuisance-assumption sensitivity (temperature 89-95 C x flow floor 0.02-0.10): across all 12 cells the 12-fraction trajectory keeps discriminating the rate (range ratio 1.9-2.0x, min MAPE 26.2-27.0%, best rate in {0.4, 0.6}) while the single integrated cup NEVER does. The localization conclusion is INVARIANT to the brew-temperature and flow-floor assumptions; the best-rate POINT shifts by 0.2 across the grid, which is expected (temperature rescales the kinetic level, not the cup-vs-fraction contrast).
external_waszkiewicz_sensitivity.strength: external-panel robustness (declared nuisance assumptions); NOT independent named-solute identification

full_cup_discrepancy.verdict: MODEL-DISCREPANCY positive control (truth generated at T+4 C, flow x1.10; fit at nominal T/flow, sweeping rate only): the fraction objective still discriminates sharply (range ratio ~7x) and, at this discrepancy magnitude, the located rate stays ROBUST near the true 1.0 (biased off 1.0 in only 7% of seeds). The discrepancy signature is instead the IRREDUCIBLE MAPE FLOOR ~4.8% -- well above the 3% noise and NOT fittable by rate alone. Conclusion: a sharp minimum near the true rate does NOT certify correct kinetics; the residual floor is the misspecification tell, and larger T/flow errors both raise the floor and begin to bias the located rate (see the larger-dose run).
full_cup_discrepancy.strength: model-discrepancy simulation positive control (seeded noise distribution); demonstrates a sharp minimum is necessary-not-sufficient

full_cup_discrepancy_large.verdict: MODEL-DISCREPANCY positive control (truth generated at T+8 C, flow x1.25; fit at nominal T/flow, sweeping rate only): the fraction objective still discriminates (range ratio ~6x) but leaves an IRREDUCIBLE MAPE floor ~5.3% (>> the 3% noise) and BIASES the located rate off the true 1.0 in 100% of seeds. A sharp fraction-scoring minimum is NECESSARY but NOT SUFFICIENT for correct kinetics -- unmodelled T/flow discrepancy shifts the located rate and cannot be fit away.
full_cup_discrepancy_large.strength: model-discrepancy simulation positive control (seeded noise distribution); demonstrates a sharp minimum is necessary-not-sufficient

continuous_optimiser.method: scipy bounded Brent on continuous log-rate profiled SSE; 10% band edges by bisection (grid-free)

identifiability_convergence.verdict: Across rate grids of 18/36/72/144 points the log-parameter SSE condition number and the domain-independent profile log-width are stable (kappa stable=True, log-width stable=True), and the flat valley persists on a narrower [0.3,3] and a wider [0.1,10] domain. A GRID-FREE continuous Brent optimiser on the profiled SSE places the rate optimum at 0.66 (interior=True) with a 10%-band log half-width of 1.43 (right-censored) -- so the practical non-identifiability is NOT an artefact of a coarse grid, the chosen sweep domain, or grid discretisation. Threshold sets that reach a sweep boundary are flagged right-censored per config.

endpoint_mass_sensitivity.verdict: HONEST endpoint-mass caveat (review A2-09): across matched-cup endpoints 38/40/42 mL (the 40 +/- 2 g window at ~unit density), the overall blind transfer MAPE is MODERATELY endpoint-sensitive -- it moves 5.3 pp (19.9 -> 25.2%) -- and the finer trigonelline-hurts signature is NOT invariant (it flips near the +5% endpoint). The ROBUST parts are the large per-condition transfer residual itself and the caffeine inventory-match improvement (both hold at every endpoint). So the headline (a large, structured residual not fixed by inventory alone) does not hinge on the 40 g approximation, but the exact MAPE magnitude and the trigonelline detail DO carry a ~5 pp endpoint uncertainty that the manuscript should state, not dismiss.
endpoint_mass_sensitivity.strength: endpoint-mass robustness of the per-condition transfer (declared 40 g approximation); does not re-open the independent-identification scope

transfer_skill.verdict: NULL-BENCHMARK skill (review A3-01): the mechanistic O->C/F transfer gives pooled held-out MAPE 8.23% vs 8.59% for an O-trained level-only constant (skill 4.2%) and 10.79% for a same-(T,p) O lookup (skill 23.8%). The model is WORSE than the constant on 50 of 108 held-out points, and the paired mean model-minus-constant loss is -0.36 pp. READING: low absolute C/F MAPE does NOT by itself establish mechanistic transfer skill -- a constant carrying only the O level is nearly competitive; the incremental skill of the kinetic/transport structure over a level-only baseline is small and must be reported alongside the absolute error, not in place of it.
transfer_skill.strength: predeclared null-benchmark skill comparison (level-only baselines, full precision); quantifies incremental mechanistic skill, not just absolute fit
